trajectory_summary: The call is a checklist run that never builds energy. Bucky completes most of the verify items efficiently but skips rapport, misses the intro legitimacy opportunity, and fails to capitalize on the call's one genuine engagement signal — Hannah's unprompted Tesla question. The bill-collection impasse, which was resolvable via verbal read-back, instead becomes the exit ramp. The call ends with a soft callback promise and no locked appointment.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is unknown, but the observable outcome is an unbooked call with a conditional callback — not a sit. Bucky's closing line ('I'll call you, and then we'll schedule you') is a soft callback, not a locked appointment. Per the schema's soft_outcome_distinction directive, this is a materially weaker outcome than a booked sit and should not be tagged as a booking.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Bill-collection pivot discipline — verbal read-back path
primary_coaching_focus.quote: Okay. What I have to do?
primary_coaching_focus.why: Hannah signaled explicit compliance at 01:50 and Bucky directed that compliance into a dead end (photo from a hospital). The verbal read-back path in bill_collection.alternative_paths resolves this in under 60 seconds. This is the single moment where the call's outcome was determined — a pivot here books the sit; the failure to pivot produces an unbooked soft callback. This is the rep's highest-leverage skill gap on this call.

key_moments.quote: Hey. It's Bucky with ION reaching out about the solar for me for the online.
key_moments.stewart_read: Bucky uses the standard/online-form intro. Hannah's question at 01:24 ('are you able to ask? About Tesla, solar. Right?') confirms this is a Tesla-sourced lead. The Tesla-certified framing — 'We're the certified solar installer for Tesla in [state]' — was the higher-legitimacy opener available here and was not used. The standard intro leaves the legitimacy question open for the first 80 seconds of the call.

Hannah begins to name a co-title holder and is cut off. Bucky's follow-up ('Oh, sorry. You went quiet. What was that?') does not recover the answer — Hannah's response at 00:34 is inaudible and the thread is dropped entirely. The verify item for co-title holders is never completed. This is a direct protocol gap: if a co-decision-maker is on the title, they must be included in the appointment per the schema.

key_moments.quote: Oh, are you able to ask? About Tesla, solar. Right?
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the call's only customer-initiated engagement signal. Hannah is asking whether she can get information about Tesla solar — unprompted, mid-qualification. This is the emotional anchor the schema identifies as the most important verify item. Bucky's response ('Yeah. The yeah. We're partnered with Tesla. Yes.') is a one-line confirmation that drops the thread and moves immediately to bill collection. The curiosity that was just offered was not developed.

key_moments.quote: I don't know. I need to work in the hospital right now.
key_moments.stewart_read: Hannah discloses she is at a hospital and cannot access her bill. This is the pivot trigger for the verbal read-back path or the 'defer to specialist on-site' path defined in the schema. Bucky's next move at 02:01 is to restate the photo requirement ('We need a picture of it for the specialist to look at, of that graph') — the opposite of a pivot. The constraint was disclosed and the rep did not adapt.
